title: Find the best CIAM solution for your business
description: Answer a few questions about your use case and we'll recommend the best identity providers for you.

steps.title: Who are your users?
steps.subtitle: This helps us match vendors to your identity use case.

options.label: Consumers / Customers (B2C)
options.description: End-users signing up for your product — social login, passkeys, magic links.

options.label: Businesses / Enterprise customers (B2B)
options.description: Teams and organisations — enterprise SSO, SAML, multi-tenancy.

options.label: Employees / Workforce (B2E)
options.description: Internal users — Active Directory, SAML, adaptive MFA.

options.label: Multiple audiences
options.description: A mix of the above — we'll balance across all dimensions.

steps.title: What's your expected scale?
steps.subtitle: Monthly active users (MAU) affect pricing and platform choice significantly.

options.label: Under 1,000 MAU
options.description: Early-stage / prototype — free tiers usually cover this.

options.label: 1,000 – 10,000 MAU
options.description: Growing startup — most free tiers still apply.

options.label: 10,000 – 100,000 MAU
options.description: Scaling product — pricing starts to matter.

options.label: 100,000 – 1,000,000 MAU
options.description: High-growth — enterprise plans or self-hosted become cost-effective.

options.label: Over 1,000,000 MAU
options.description: Large-scale — custom contracts or self-hosted typically required.

steps.title: How do you want to run it?
steps.subtitle: Deployment model affects control, compliance, and total cost.

options.label: Cloud SaaS
options.description: Fully managed — no infrastructure to operate, fastest to start.

options.label: Self-hosted
options.description: You run it — full data control, ideal for strict compliance or cost at scale.

options.label: Either works
options.description: Open to both — we'll factor in other dimensions more.

steps.title: Which capabilities matter most?
steps.subtitle: Select all that apply. We'll score vendors on how well they support these.

options.label: Enterprise SSO
options.description: SAML 2.0, per-customer SSO connections for B2B.

options.label: Passkeys / WebAuthn
options.description: Phishing-resistant, password-free authentication.

options.label: Social Login
options.description: Sign in with Google, Apple, GitHub, and more.

options.label: Passwordless / Magic Links
options.description: One-time codes and magic links via email or SMS.

options.label: Adaptive / Risk-Based MFA
options.description: MFA triggered by anomalous signals (device, location, IP).

options.label: Machine-to-Machine (M2M)
options.description: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials for service-to-service auth.

options.label: Custom Branding
options.description: Fully customisable login pages and email templates.

steps.title: Any regulatory requirements?
steps.subtitle: Select all that apply. Vendors without the required certifications will be ranked lower.

options.label: SOC 2 Type II
options.description: Security and availability audits for SaaS companies.

options.label: GDPR / EU Data Residency
options.description: European data protection regulation compliance.

options.label: HIPAA
options.description: US healthcare data protection — requires a BAA.

options.label: FedRAMP
options.description: US federal government cloud authorization.

options.label: PCI DSS
options.description: Payment card industry data security standard.

options.label: ISO 27001
options.description: International information security management standard.

steps.title: What's your monthly budget?
steps.subtitle: This helps filter out vendors that may be out of range for your stage.

options.label: Free / Open Source
options.description: Looking for free tiers or self-hostable open-source solutions.

options.label: Under $100 / month
options.description: Early-stage budget — starter plans.

options.label: $100 – $1,000 / month
options.description: Growth-stage — professional plans.

options.label: Enterprise budget
options.description: Custom pricing negotiable — scale and SLAs matter more than cost.
